Plain Prophecy

Biblical Prophecy.
Plain and Simple.

What the Reformers believed — rigorously examined. Every prophetic anchor verified against history. Christ at the centre of every timeline.

Start Comparing →Our Mission
“Seal not the sayings of the prophecy of this book: for the time is at hand.”
Revelation 22:10
Evidence

Why the Historicist Framework Stands

📜
Verified to the Year
Daniel's 70 Weeks

457 BC → 27 AD → 31 AD → 34 AD. Every data point in Daniel 9 is confirmed by independent archaeology and secular chronology. No other prophetic system matches this precision.

Dan 9:24–27
⏳
Verified to the Decade
The 1,260 Years

Six independent Bible texts point to the same period. Applied historically: 538 AD (Justinian) to 1798 AD (Napoleon captures the Pope) = exactly 1,260 years. This is not coincidence.

Dan 7:25 · Rev 12:6, 14 · Rev 13:5
☁
One Event — Every Eye Sees
The Second Coming

"Every eye shall see Him" (Rev 1:7). "The Lord himself shall descend with a shout" (1 Thess 4:16). Scripture describes one visible, glorious, audible return of Christ — not two secret/public stages invented in 1830.

Rev 1:7 · 1 Thess 4:16–17

The Reformation Consensus

Luther, Calvin, Newton, and Wesley All Agreed

The Historicist reading of Daniel and Revelation was the dominant Protestant position for 300 years. Evangelical futurism is a 19th-century innovation with Counter-Reformation origins. The evidence is not close.

See the Full Comparison →
